title: External Asset Repository via S3 + Local Cache
description: Define and implement the external game-asset workflow for large binary models that should not live in git. Assets are stored in S3 and synced to a local asset repository path configured by environment variable. Engine loading paths should resolve model files from that local repo for runtime usage.
definition-of-done: 1. Environment variable `IXDAR_ASSET_REPO_ROOT` is the source of truth for local asset repo location. 2. A documented S3 path convention exists for shared assets and versioned folders. 3. Sync workflow from S3 to local repo is documented (manual or scripted). 4. Platform/File loading can load model files from local asset repo. 5. Model test scene successfully loads `Hand.obj` from local asset repo. 6. Clear error messaging when environment variable or file is missing.
testing-plan: 1. Manual Test: Set `IXDAR_ASSET_REPO_ROOT` to local asset repo and verify `Hand.obj` loads in model test scene. 2. Negative Test: Unset env var and verify clear startup/load error. 3. Negative Test: Keep env var set but remove `Hand.obj` and verify missing-file error. 4. Cross-Platform Check: Verify desktop/headless loaders resolve external filesystem paths correctly.
todos: Define S3 bucket/prefix layout for model assets and versions
Document sync process from S3 to local asset repo (PowerShell and/or CLI)
Implement env-var based path resolution in file management
Implement platform support for loading external files from filesystem
Add model loading test scene that reads `Hand.obj` from local asset repo
unknowns: Exact S3 URI naming convention for environments (dev/stage/prod)
Whether sync should be done via aws cli script, custom tool, or CI artifact download
Whether assets should be checksum-validated after sync
Whether web runtime needs a parallel HTTP asset strategy instead of filesystem paths
